hypothesis: Incorporating three-dimensional conformational descriptors (e.g., serialized atomic coordinate strings or distance matrix encodings) as an eighth modality within the STR-Bamba unified vocabulary will create a distinct, geometrically-informed cluster in the latent space that improves prediction accuracy for properties critically dependent on molecular shape—such as protein-ligand binding affinity and stereochemistry-sensitive bioactivity—beyond what any combination of the existing seven two-dimensional textual representations can achieve, because the current multimodal framework lacks explicit spatial information and therefore cannot encode chirality or conformational strain through string-based notations alone.
